title: Washing soda pre-treatment stain spray
subtitle: An alkaline spray for treating food, grease, and collar stains before washing
excerpt: A pre-wash stain treatment made with washing soda and liquid castile soap dissolved in water. Spray directly onto food stains, grease marks, and collar soiling before putting clothes in the machine. Works on most protein and fat-based stains.
sourceNotes: Washing soda (sodium carbonate, pH 11.4) for stain pre-treatment: alkaline conditions saponify fats and break down protein stains (blood, food, collar soil). Standard laundry chemistry reference. Liquid castile soap as a surfactant: helps the alkaline solution penetrate fabric fibres and suspend the loosened soil for rinsing.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 spray bottle (~500 ml, approximately 40 to 60 spot treatments)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep washing soda at 2% of formula weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake gently before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store at room temperature. Use within 3 months. Washing soda solution is stable at room temperature but the castile soap can separate over time; shake before use.

glossaryTerms.definition: In laundry, washing soda's high alkalinity initiates a partial saponification of fat and oil stains, breaking them down and making them easier to rinse away in the wash cycle.

content.text: Pre-treating stains before washing significantly improves removal rates for food, grease, and collar soiling. This spray relies on washing soda's high alkalinity (pH 11.4) to begin the partial

of fat-based stains, and on liquid castile soap as a surfactant to suspend the loosened soil so the machine wash can rinse it away. The formula is dilute enough for most fabrics but should be spot-tested on delicate or pale materials before use. It works best on food and grease stains. It is not a bleach and will not remove rust stains, wine stains that have set, or dye transfer. Apply, leave 5 to 10 minutes, then wash as normal.

items.prepNote: 92% of formula; warm water helps dissolve the washing soda
3% of formula; sodium carbonate; dissolve fully before adding to bottle
5% of formula; surfactant to lift loosened soil

content.text: Dissolve the washing soda in the warm water and stir until completely clear; undissolved crystals will clog the spray nozzle. Allow the solution to cool to room temperature, then add the liquid castile soap. Stir gently to combine; do not shake vigorously as it will foam. Pour into the spray bottle.

Shake the bottle gently before use. Spray directly onto the stain from a distance of about 15 cm. For greasy or heavy stains, work the spray gently into the fabric with a soft brush or cloth. Leave for 5 to 10 minutes. Wash the item as normal in the machine. The spray works on fresh stains much more effectively than on set stains; treat as soon as possible after the stain occurs.

Suitable for cotton, linen, synthetic fabrics, and most blends. Test on a hidden area before using on delicate, pale, or undyed fabrics. Do not use on wool or silk (the alkalinity damages protein fibres) or on dry-clean-only garments.
Store at room temperature. Use within 3 months. Shake before each use.
